summary: cosmosdb-server typosquats @vercel/cosmosdb-server and beacons CI/host identity to attacker-controlled endpoint
details: Package name targets the unscoped namespace of @vercel/cosmosdb-server. When the bin script runs (e.g., via `npx cosmosdb-server`), index.js collects host identity (os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), cwd, home dir, platform/arch), enumerates all non-internal IPv4 addresses via os.networkInterfaces(), and reads CI/build identity environment variables (CI, GITHUB_ACTIONS, GITHUB_REPOSITORY, GITHUB_ACTOR, VERCEL, VERCEL_ENV, NOW_BUILDER, GITLAB_CI, JENKINS_URL, CIRCLECI, npm_lifecycle_event, npm_package_name). The collected JSON is POSTed to a hardcoded Cloudflare Worker endpoint at https://callback-monitor.cyb3rsh4ykh.workers.dev/c (index.js:15, index.js:55). Errors are swallowed and the process exits via setTimeout, hiding the beacon from the user. This is the CI/build-system fingerprinting shape used to identify high-value targets (Vercel-internal builds, GitHub Actions runners) for follow-on supply-chain attacks. The README's 'security research' framing does not constitute installer consent — anyone running the bin leaks identifying host and CI metadata to a third party.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
